title: Algorithmic News Feeds and Misinformation Susceptibility Among Young Urban News Consumers in Dhaka
desc: A sequential explanatory mixed-methods study examining how algorithmically curated social media shapes epistemic vulnerability among university students. Uses a validated survey (n=130–150) and qualitative interviews to measure the structural, behavioural, and cognitive dimensions of algorithmic misinformation exposure.
methods: Python data handling, qualitative interviews, validated media literacy survey instruments

title: SAALWatch: South Asian Algorithmic Literacy Audit
desc: A pilot platform audit framework for South Asian newsrooms. Each audit focuses on one event and one platform, combining scraped data, manual coding, and replication-ready code published on GitHub.
methods: Web scraping (Python), content analysis, reproducible notebooks

title: Algorithms, media literacy and education in Bangladesh
desc: Final-year thesis extending the 2026 pilot study into a larger, education-policy-facing argument: how curriculum design in Bangladeshi schools and universities can be rebuilt around algorithmic literacy.
methods: Document analysis, stakeholder interviews, policy review
